predictions.text: Freddie Mac’s weekly Primary Mortgage Market Survey will report the 30-year fixed-rate mortgage average at or above 7.75% in at least one weekly release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if any Freddie Mac PMMS weekly release dated on/before 2026-05-31 lists the 30-year FRM average rate >= 7.75%. Otherwise NO.
evidence: Mortgage rates jumped to a 7-month high, threatening spring home sales and affordability as financing costs re-accelerate. (CNBC, 2026-03-13)
monitoring_signals: Freddie Mac PMMS weekly 30-year FRM level
10-year Treasury yield level and volatility
MBS current-coupon spreads and rate-lock volumes
predictions.rationale: Rates have recently re-accelerated; a further move higher is plausible if term premia stay elevated and inflation progress stalls.

predictions.text: MBA’s weekly Purchase Index (4-week moving average) will be down at least 20% year-over-year in at least one weekly release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if, in any Mortgage Bankers Association weekly applications report dated on/before 2026-06-30, the purchase index 4-week moving average is <= -20% YoY (as stated or computable from published YoY figure). Otherwise NO.
evidence: Mortgage rates jumped to a 7-month high, threatening spring home sales and affordability as financing costs re-accelerate. (CNBC, 2026-03-13)
monitoring_signals: MBA Weekly Applications Survey (purchase index YoY, 4-week avg)
Freddie Mac PMMS mortgage rates
Homebuilder incentives and inventory measures
predictions.rationale: Higher financing costs typically transmit quickly to purchase applications, especially during the spring selling season.

predictions.text: NAR will report existing-home sales at or below 3.80 million SAAR in at least one monthly release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if any National Association of Realtors Existing-Home Sales release dated on/before 2026-07-31 reports SAAR <= 3.80 million for the reported month. Otherwise NO.
evidence: Mortgage rates jumped to a 7-month high, threatening spring home sales and affordability as financing costs re-accelerate. (CNBC, 2026-03-13)
U.S. Q4 GDP revised down to 0.7% annualized while January core inflation ran 3.1%. (CNBC, 2026-03-13)
monitoring_signals: NAR pending-home-sales index and existing-home sales SAAR
Mortgage purchase applications and rate-lock data
Months’ supply and median price trends
predictions.rationale: Affordability strain plus softer growth increases odds of a weak existing-sales print before peak season ends.

predictions.text: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield will close at or above 5.25% on at least one trading day (UST 10Y constant maturity, market close).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if the 10-year Treasury yield (e.g., Treasury.gov daily yield or widely cited 10Y benchmark close) is reported at a market close >= 5.25% on any trading day on/before 2026-06-30. Otherwise NO.
evidence: Investors are reportedly shunning U.S. debt as a safe haven during the Iran conflict, implying rising risk/term premia. (MarketWatch, 2026-03-13)
U.S. Q4 GDP revised down to 0.7% annualized while January core inflation ran 3.1%. (CNBC, 2026-03-13)
monitoring_signals: 10Y yield daily closes; term premium estimates
Inflation surprise indices; CPI/PCE releases
Auction demand metrics and foreign official flows
predictions.rationale: If haven demand is impaired and inflation remains sticky, term premia could push 10Y yields to new highs despite slower growth.

predictions.text: At least one U.S. Treasury nominal coupon auction (10-year or 30-year) will show both a high-yield tail of at least 2.0 bps and a bid-to-cover ratio of 2.25 or lower.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if any Treasury 10Y or 30Y nominal auction result published on TreasuryDirect on/before 2026-06-30 has (i) high yield minus when-issued yield >= 0.020% (2.0 bps) and (ii) bid-to-cover <= 2.25. Otherwise NO.
evidence: Investors are reportedly shunning U.S. debt as a safe haven during the Iran conflict, implying rising risk/term premia. (MarketWatch, 2026-03-13)
Mortgage rates jumped to a 7-month high, consistent with rising long-end yields. (CNBC, 2026-03-13)
monitoring_signals: Treasury auction tails, bid-to-cover, indirect/direct bidder shares
Dealer balance-sheet usage and repo specialness
MOVE index and rate-vol levels
predictions.rationale: Weaker marginal demand plus higher rate volatility raises the probability of a poorly absorbed long-end auction.

predictions.text: ICE BofA US High Yield Index Option-Adjusted Spread (OAS) will close at or above 600 bps on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if ICE BofA US High Yield Index OAS (e.g., FRED series BAMLH0A0HYM2) is reported at a daily close >= 6.00 (600 bps) on/before 2026-06-30. Otherwise NO.
evidence: Oil closed above $100 for a second day, up ~40% since the war began; supply-risk premiums persist. (CNBC, 2026-03-13)
U.S. Q4 GDP revised down to 0.7% annualized. (CNBC, 2026-03-13)
monitoring_signals: HY OAS (FRED/ICE) and CCC spreads
Default-rate trackers and distressed-debt ratios
Equity volatility and funding spreads
predictions.rationale: Geopolitical energy shock plus slowing growth increases downside tail risk for credit, though base case remains below major stress levels.

predictions.text: At least one major semiconductor manufacturer (Intel, TSMC, Samsung Electronics, SK hynix, Micron, Texas Instruments, or GlobalFoundries) will explicitly cite helium supply disruption/constraints tied to Qatar in an earnings call transcript or SEC filing.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if, on/before 2026-05-31, an earnings call transcript or an SEC filing (10-Q/10-K/8-K or equivalent) from any named firm explicitly mentions helium supply disruption/constraints and links it to Qatar (e.g., 'Qatar helium', 'Qatari helium shutdown'). Otherwise NO.
evidence: Qatar helium shutdown could disrupt semiconductor supply chains within weeks. (Tom's Hardware, 2026-03-13)
monitoring_signals: Company earnings-call transcripts (keyword: helium, Qatar)
Industrial gas supplier updates (Air Liquide, Linde, Air Products)
Semiconductor fab utilization and lead-time commentary
predictions.rationale: If the shutdown persists, it is likely to show up quickly in supply-chain risk disclosures or Q&A.

predictions.text: A U.S. hyperscaler (Alphabet/Google, Amazon/AWS, Microsoft, Meta, or Apple) will publicly announce a nuclear-power offtake agreement for data centers with contracted capacity of at least 300 MW (nameplate or contracted).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if, on/before 2026-12-31, any named firm issues a press release, SEC filing, or official blog post announcing a nuclear energy offtake/PPA (existing nuclear or SMR) for data centers with stated contracted capacity >= 300 MW. Otherwise NO.
evidence: AI data-center infrastructure expansion is accelerating (Eaton acquisition; Nvidia GTC focus on compute/memory/network constraints). (CNBC, 2026-03-13)
monitoring_signals: Company sustainability/energy procurement announcements
Utility/IPP press releases and interconnection filings
Data-center load forecasts and regional grid constraints
predictions.rationale: AI-driven power demand and grid constraints make large, firm clean power contracts increasingly attractive and politically supportable.

predictions.text: Iran’s crude oil exports will average at least 1.0 million barrels per day lower than their February 2026 average for a continuous 4-week period, as estimated by at least one major tanker-tracking provider (Kpler or Vortexa) or the IEA.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if Kpler, Vortexa, or IEA publications/data cited publicly on/before 2026-06-30 show a continuous 4-week average Iran crude export level that is >=1.0 mb/d below the provider’s stated/derived February 2026 average. Otherwise NO.
evidence: U.S. strikes hit Iran’s Kharg Island military targets; escalation risk around shipping lanes persists. (NYT, 2026-03-13)
Oil up ~40% since the war began; supply-risk premiums persist. (CNBC, 2026-03-13)
monitoring_signals: Kpler/Vortexa Iran export estimates and loading activity near Kharg
AIS disruptions, insurance and shipping route changes
OPEC/IEA monthly supply commentary
predictions.rationale: Kharg-related disruption or heightened sanctions/interdictions could materially reduce exports for at least a month.

predictions.text: BLS CPI will show CPI Shelter (seasonally adjusted) rising less than 3.0% year-over-year in at least one monthly CPI release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if any BLS CPI release dated on/before 2026-12-31 reports CPI-U Shelter index (or Shelter component) YoY inflation < 3.0% (not rounded up). Otherwise NO.
evidence: Growth has softened (Q4 GDP revised down), while inflation remains a policy constraint. (CNBC, 2026-03-13)
monitoring_signals: BLS CPI shelter and OER month-over-month trends
Zillow/Apartment List rent indices (lead indicators)
Vacancy rates and new lease rent growth
predictions.rationale: New-lease rent measures have tended to lead CPI shelter; slower growth and affordability pressure increase odds of continued shelter disinflation.
